title: Full Kitchen Remodel

budget.description: Cosmetic refresh - paint cabinets, new hardware, laminate countertops, budget appliances, LVP flooring

midRange.description: New semi-custom cabinets, quartz countertops, mid-range appliances, tile backsplash, hardwood flooring

premium.description: Custom cabinets, marble or premium quartz countertops, professional-grade appliances, custom lighting, structural changes

costFactors.title: Cabinet Selection
costFactors.description: Cabinets eat 30-40% of your total budget. Stock cabinets from a big-box store run $100-$300 per linear foot installed. Semi-custom cabinets jump to $150-$650 per linear foot. Full custom work from a cabinet shop runs $500-$1,200 per linear foot. The average kitchen has 25-30 linear feet of cabinets, so this decision alone can swing your budget by $15,000 or more.

costFactors.title: Countertop Material
costFactors.description: Laminate countertops run $20-$50 per square foot installed. Granite and quartz fall in the $50-$200 range. Marble pushes $100-$250 per square foot. For a typical kitchen with 35 square feet of counter space, the difference between laminate and mid-range quartz is roughly $2,000-$4,000.

costFactors.title: Layout Changes
costFactors.description: Moving plumbing, gas lines, or electrical runs adds significant cost. Relocating a sink costs $400-$2,000. Moving a gas line runs $375-$750. Removing a wall to create an open concept layout costs $1,400-$11,500 depending on whether it's load-bearing. If you can keep your existing layout, you save thousands.

costFactors.title: Appliance Package
costFactors.description: A basic appliance set (fridge, range, dishwasher, microwave) starts around $1,200-$2,100. Mid-range stainless packages run $2,500-$6,000. Professional-grade appliances from brands like Sub-Zero, Wolf, or Thermador can push $13,000-$25,000 for a full kitchen suite.

costFactors.title: Kitchen Size
costFactors.description: A small galley kitchen (75 sq ft) might remodel for $5,600-$17,500. A standard 10x10 kitchen (100 sq ft) runs $15,000-$45,000 for a mid-range job. Large kitchens over 200 sq ft can easily exceed $50,000. Cost per square foot generally ranges from $75 to $250.

materialOptions.cons: Less durable, can chip, lower resale impact

materialOptions.pros: Natural stone, heat resistant, high-end look
materialOptions.cons: Requires sealing every 1-3 years, can crack

materialOptions.pros: Low maintenance, consistent color, very durable
materialOptions.bestFor: Most popular mid-range to premium choice

materialOptions.pros: Luxury aesthetic, unique veining, high resale value
materialOptions.cons: Stains easily, scratches, requires significant maintenance
Requires monthly oiling, can harbor bacteria if not maintained

westCoast.label: West Coast
westCoast.note: Highest labor costs nationally, strict permitting

midwest.label: Midwest
midwest.note: Lowest costs nationally, good contractor availability

diyVsPro.diyRisks: Plumbing mistakes can cause water damage costing thousands. Electrical errors are a fire hazard. Improper cabinet installation voids manufacturer warranties and can result in sagging or falling cabinets.

savingsTips: Keep your existing layout. Moving plumbing and electrical is where costs spike - a same-footprint remodel saves $3,000-$10,000.
Reface cabinets instead of replacing them. If your cabinet boxes are solid, refacing costs $4,000-$10,000 vs. $12,000-$30,000 for new cabinets.
Choose stock cabinets over custom. Ready-to-assemble (RTA) cabinets from IKEA or big-box stores save 30-50% vs. custom.
Time your purchase around holiday sales. Memorial Day, Black Friday, and end-of-year clearance events offer 20-40% off appliances.
Use mid-range quartz instead of marble. Quartz delivers a similar look at $50-$100/sq ft vs. marble at $100-$250, and requires zero maintenance.
Do your own demolition. Ripping out old cabinets and flooring saves $500-$2,000 in labor, and it's straightforward work.
Skip the kitchen island if space is tight. Islands under 4x6 feet often aren't worth the $3,000-$8,000 investment.
Choose a single-basin undermount sink. They're $150-$400 and look premium without the $800+ price tag of specialty sinks.

contractorQuestions.question: What's included in your quote - and what's not?
contractorQuestions.why: Some contractors exclude permits, appliance installation, or dumpster fees. Getting this in writing prevents surprise costs.

contractorQuestions.question: How do you handle unexpected issues like water damage or outdated wiring?
contractorQuestions.why: Hidden problems are common behind old cabinets. You need to know their policy for change orders before signing.

contractorQuestions.question: Can I see 3 recent kitchen projects you've completed?
contractorQuestions.why: Photos and references for similar-scope projects tell you more than any sales pitch.

contractorQuestions.question: Who will be on-site daily, and who is my point of contact?
contractorQuestions.why: Many contractors run multiple jobs. You want to know who's actually supervising your project.

contractorQuestions.question: What's your payment schedule?
contractorQuestions.why: Red flag if they want more than 10-15% upfront. Standard is a deposit, then progress payments tied to milestones.

contractorQuestions.question: Are you licensed, insured, and bonded?
contractorQuestions.why: Verify their license number with your state licensing board. Insurance protects you if a worker is injured in your home.

contractorQuestions.question: What warranty do you offer on labor?
contractorQuestions.why: Most reputable contractors offer 1-2 year warranties on workmanship. Material warranties are separate.

secondaryKeywords: how much does a kitchen remodel cost
seo.metaDescription: How much does a kitchen remodel cost in 2026? National average is $27,000. Get detailed cost breakdowns by tier, material comparisons, regional pricing, and an interactive calculator.
